claim: Vesper is a formally verified microkernel whose functional correctness is proved down to the level of its C implementation using an interactive theorem prover.

supporting_evidence.quote: Vesper is a formally verified microkernel whose functional correctness is proved down to the level of its C implementation using an interactive theorem prover.

claim: The verification establishes that Vesper's implementation refines its abstract specification: every behavior the implementation can exhibit is already permitted by the specification.

supporting_evidence.quote: The verification establishes that Vesper's implementation refines its abstract specification: every behavior the implementation can exhibit is already permitted by the specification.

claim: The proof is organized into several refinement layers: an abstract specification, an executable but still abstract design, and finally the concrete C implementation.

supporting_evidence.quote: The proof is organized into several refinement layers: an abstract specification, an executable but still abstract design, and finally the concrete C implementation.

claim: Each layer is proved to refine the layer above it, so correctness of the full stack follows from composing three separate, more tractable refinement proofs rather than one enormous proof.

supporting_evidence.quote: Each layer is proved to refine the layer above it, so correctness of the full stack follows from composing three separate, more tractable refinement proofs rather than one enormous proof.

claim: A separate proof layer establishes integrity: no user-level process can modify kernel memory or the memory of another process without going through an authorized kernel mechanism.

supporting_evidence.quote: A separate proof layer establishes integrity: no user-level process can modify kernel memory or the memory of another process without going through an authorized kernel mechanism.

claim: Vesper's non-interference proof took roughly three times as long to develop as the functional correctness proof it depends on, reflecting this added difficulty.

supporting_evidence.quote: Vesper's non-interference proof took roughly three times as long to develop as the functional correctness proof it depends on, reflecting this added difficulty.

claim: The full Vesper verification effort, from initial specification to a complete, machine-checked proof, consumed approximately 25 person-years of proof engineering labor.

supporting_evidence.quote: The full Vesper verification effort, from initial specification to a complete, machine-checked proof, consumed approximately 25 person-years of proof engineering labor.

claim: A significant fraction of the effort, closer to a third by some internal estimates, went into proof maintenance: updating existing proofs after a specification or implementation change rather than proving new properties.

supporting_evidence.quote: A significant fraction of the effort, closer to a third by some internal estimates, went into proof maintenance: updating existing proofs after a specification or implementation change rather than proving new properties.

claim: Formal verification proves properties about a model of the system, and any gap between that model and physical reality is a potential source of real-world failure the proof does not cover.

supporting_evidence.quote: Formal verification proves properties about a model of the system, and any gap between that model and physical reality is a potential source of real-world failure the proof does not cover.

claim: Side-channel attacks, which exploit physical characteristics like timing or power consumption rather than the kernel's logical behavior, are also outside the scope of the functional-correctness and non-interference proofs.

supporting_evidence.quote: Side-channel attacks, which exploit physical characteristics like timing or power consumption rather than the kernel's logical behavior, are also outside the scope of the functional-correctness and non-interference proofs.

claim: Vesper has been adopted primarily in safety- and security-critical embedded deployments, including avionics subsystems and hardware security modules, where the verification's upfront cost is easier to justify.

supporting_evidence.quote: Vesper has been adopted primarily in safety- and security-critical embedded deployments, including avionics subsystems and hardware security modules, where the verification's upfront cost is easier to justify.

claim: This split means Vesper's formal guarantees cover only the kernel itself; a deployment's overall security still depends heavily on the correctness of everything built on top of it.

supporting_evidence.quote: This split means Vesper's formal guarantees cover only the kernel itself; a deployment's overall security still depends heavily on the correctness of everything built on top of it.

claim: Vesper's first complete, machine-checked functional correctness proof was published in 2011, covering an implementation of roughly 9,000 lines of C code.

supporting_evidence.quote: Vesper's first complete, machine-checked functional correctness proof was published in 2011, covering an implementation of roughly 9,000 lines of C code.

claim: The non-interference proof, covering the same implementation, followed in 2013, after the additional verification effort described in the verified properties section.

supporting_evidence.quote: The non-interference proof, covering the same implementation, followed in 2013, after the additional verification effort described in the verified properties section.

claim: A significant kernel redesign in 2017 required re-proving large portions of the functional correctness layer, though the refinement-layer architecture meant the abstract specification proofs needed only modest adjustment.

supporting_evidence.quote: A significant kernel redesign in 2017 required re-proving large portions of the functional correctness layer, though the refinement-layer architecture meant the abstract specification proofs needed only modest adjustment.

claim: Formal verification techniques have also been applied to zero-knowledge proof systems, including a widely cited study that formally verified a circuit implementation of a Groth-Delta-style proving system.

supporting_evidence.quote: Formal verification techniques have also been applied to zero-knowledge proof systems, including a widely cited study that formally verified a circuit implementation of a Groth-Delta-style proving system.

claim: That study's own instantiation of the trusted-setup ceremony, run as part of validating the formally verified circuit end-to-end, recorded 175 participants contributing randomness to the reference string.

supporting_evidence.quote: That study's own instantiation of the trusted-setup ceremony, run as part of validating the formally verified circuit end-to-end, recorded 175 participants contributing randomness to the reference string.

claim: The formal verification in that study covered the circuit's constraint system, proving it correctly encoded the intended computation, but did not itself verify the ceremony's cryptographic security, which was analyzed separately.

supporting_evidence.quote: The formal verification in that study covered the circuit's constraint system, proving it correctly encoded the intended computation, but did not itself verify the ceremony's cryptographic security, which was analyzed separately.

claim: A separate formal-verification effort applied similar refinement-proof techniques to a compiler's intermediate representation optimization passes, targeting a specification published by the Common IR Working Group, CIRWG.

supporting_evidence.quote: A separate formal-verification effort applied similar refinement-proof techniques to a compiler's intermediate representation optimization passes, targeting a specification published by the Common IR Working Group, CIRWG.

claim: The CIRWG v1 specification was ratified in 2019, and the verified-compiler-passes project used that ratified specification as the formal target its refinement proofs were checked against.

supporting_evidence.quote: The CIRWG v1 specification was ratified in 2019, and the verified-compiler-passes project used that ratified specification as the formal target its refinement proofs were checked against.

claim: The project proved that several common optimization passes, constant folding and dead-code elimination among them, preserve program semantics exactly as CIRWG v1 defines them.

supporting_evidence.quote: The project proved that several common optimization passes, constant folding and dead-code elimination among them, preserve program semantics exactly as CIRWG v1 defines them.

claim: Person-years of proof engineering effort break down unevenly across Vesper's three refinement layers.

supporting_evidence.quote: Person-years of proof engineering effort break down unevenly across Vesper's three refinement layers.

claim: The C implementation layer's disproportionate share of both effort and proof size reflects how much more detailed reasoning is required once the proof must track concrete memory layouts and pointer arithmetic.

supporting_evidence.quote: The C implementation layer's disproportionate share of both effort and proof size reflects how much more detailed reasoning is required once the proof must track concrete memory layouts and pointer arithmetic.

claim: The 2017 kernel redesign's proof-maintenance cost varied sharply by layer, illustrating the refinement architecture's intended benefit.

supporting_evidence.quote: The 2017 kernel redesign's proof-maintenance cost varied sharply by layer, illustrating the refinement architecture's intended benefit.

claim: The abstract specification's comparatively small rework cost is precisely the payoff the layered refinement architecture was designed to deliver.

supporting_evidence.quote: The abstract specification's comparatively small rework cost is precisely the payoff the layered refinement architecture was designed to deliver.

claim: Before a proof is merged into Vesper's verified codebase, reviewers work through the following checklist.

supporting_evidence.quote: Before a proof is merged into Vesper's verified codebase, reviewers work through the following checklist.

claim: The third item, checking for convenience axioms, catches the most subtle class of error, since an unproven axiom can silently make an otherwise correct-looking proof vacuous.

supporting_evidence.quote: The third item, checking for convenience axioms, catches the most subtle class of error, since an unproven axiom can silently make an otherwise correct-looking proof vacuous.

claim: New proof engineers joining the Vesper project complete the following onboarding steps before working on production proofs.

supporting_evidence.quote: New proof engineers joining the Vesper project complete the following onboarding steps before working on production proofs.

claim: New engineers typically require between two and four months to become fully independently productive, according to the project's own onboarding data.

supporting_evidence.quote: New engineers typically require between two and four months to become fully independently productive, according to the project's own onboarding data.

claim: Yamada and Kimura's 精整関係に基づくオペレーティングシステムカーネルの形式検証 (2009) proposes an early refinement framework that Vesper's proof architecture directly builds on.

supporting_evidence.quote: Yamada and Kimura's 精整関係に基づくオペレーティングシステムカーネルの形式検証 (2009) proposes an early refinement framework that Vesper's proof architecture directly builds on.

claim: Fedorova's Верификация невмешательства в защищённых операционных системах (2012) formalizes the non-interference property Vesper's third proof layer establishes.

supporting_evidence.quote: Fedorova's Верификация невмешательства в защищённых операционных системах (2012) formalizes the non-interference property Vesper's third proof layer establishes.

claim: Formally, C refines A when there exists a relation R such that for every reachable concrete state c and abstract state a with R(c, a), every transition c -> c' in C is matched by some transition a -> a' in A with R(c', a').

supporting_evidence.quote: Formally, C refines A when there exists a relation R such that for every reachable concrete state c and abstract state a with R(c, a), every transition c -> c' in C is matched by some transition a -> a' in A with R(c', a').

claim: This transitivity is precisely what allows a security property proved once against the abstract specification A to be inherited automatically by the concrete implementation C without a separate proof.

supporting_evidence.quote: This transitivity is precisely what allows a security property proved once against the abstract specification A to be inherited automatically by the concrete implementation C without a separate proof.

claim: The functional correctness proof for the production-scale kernel took approximately twelve person-years, delivered in 2011, roughly six times the effort of the original simplified prototype despite the production kernel being only about twenty times larger in code size.

supporting_evidence.quote: The functional correctness proof for the production-scale kernel took approximately twelve person-years, delivered in 2011, roughly six times the effort of the original simplified prototype despite the production kernel being only about twenty times larger in code size.

claim: The non-interference proof that followed in 2013 required extending the theorem prover's own proof libraries with new reasoning principles about information flow, since the existing libraries built for functional correctness proofs were not sufficient on their own.

supporting_evidence.quote: The non-interference proof that followed in 2013 required extending the theorem prover's own proof libraries with new reasoning principles about information flow, since the existing libraries built for functional correctness proofs were not sufficient on their own.

claim: The project's own retrospective materials describe the initial simplified-prototype phase as the single most important early decision, arguing that attempting the production-scale proof directly, without first validating feasibility on a much smaller system, would very likely have failed or taken substantially longer.

supporting_evidence.quote: The project's own retrospective materials describe the initial simplified-prototype phase as the single most important early decision, arguing that attempting the production-scale proof directly, without first validating feasibility on a much smaller system, would very likely have failed or taken substantially longer.

claim: In 2018, seven years after the functional correctness proof was first published, an external security researcher reported a behavior in a deployed Vesper-based system that appeared to contradict the kernel's proven security guarantees.

supporting_evidence.quote: In 2018, seven years after the functional correctness proof was first published, an external security researcher reported a behavior in a deployed Vesper-based system that appeared to contradict the kernel's proven security guarantees.

claim: This was, precisely, an instance of the specification-completeness gap the project's own documentation had warned about from the beginning: the proof showed the implementation matched its specification, not that the specification captured every property a user might reasonably expect.

supporting_evidence.quote: This was, precisely, an instance of the specification-completeness gap the project's own documentation had warned about from the beginning: the proof showed the implementation matched its specification, not that the specification captured every property a user might reasonably expect.

claim: The full fix, including specification extension and complete re-verification of the affected proof layers, took approximately five person-months, substantially longer than a comparable fix would take in an unverified system, but the project's maintainers noted the fix came with a machine-checked guarantee the gap could not recur through the same mechanism.

supporting_evidence.quote: The full fix, including specification extension and complete re-verification of the affected proof layers, took approximately five person-months, substantially longer than a comparable fix would take in an unverified system, but the project's maintainers noted the fix came with a machine-checked guarantee the gap could not recur through the same mechanism.

claim: This recap restates Vesper's verification approach for readers skimming only the overview section.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap restates Vesper's verification approach for readers skimming only the overview section.

claim: The full proof architecture, spanning three refinement layers, is described in complete detail in its own dedicated section rather than repeated here.

supporting_evidence.quote: The full proof architecture, spanning three refinement layers, is described in complete detail in its own dedicated section rather than repeated here.

claim: This recap restates the functional correctness proof's publication for readers who only skim the version history section.

supporting_evidence.quote: This recap restates the functional correctness proof's publication for readers who only skim the version history section.

claim: The non-interference proof followed some time later, as described in full in the version history section above.

supporting_evidence.quote: The non-interference proof followed some time later, as described in full in the version history section above.

claim: The C implementation layer consumed 15 person-years and 345,000 lines of proof.

supporting_evidence.quote: | C implementation | 15 | 345,000 |

claim: The abstract specification layer consumed 4 person-years and 45,000 lines of proof.

supporting_evidence.quote: | Abstract specification | 4 | 45,000 |

claim: The 2017 redesign changed 800 abstract-specification lines and cost 2 person-months of proof rework.

supporting_evidence.quote: | Abstract specification | 800 | 2 person-months |

claim: The 2017 redesign changed 22,000 C-implementation lines and cost 14 person-months of proof rework.

supporting_evidence.quote: | C implementation | 22,000 | 14 person-months |

claim: Refinement composes across layers because it is transitive: if C refines D and D refines A, then C refines A.

supporting_evidence.quote: Composing refinements across the three layers, abstract specification, executable design, and C implementation, follows because refinement is transitive: if C refines D and D refines A, then C refines A directly.

claim: The combined proof scripts exceed 500,000 lines, roughly fifty times the size of the verified C implementation.

supporting_evidence.quote: As of the most recent public status report, the combined proof scripts across all layers exceed 500,000 lines, roughly fifty times the size of the verified C implementation itself.

claim: CIRWG v1 enumerates 42 core IR operations that a conforming implementation must support.

supporting_evidence.quote: The ratified specification enumerates 42 core IR operations that a conforming implementation must support, all of which the verified passes handle.

claim: Proof review checks that no part of a proof relies on an unproven convenience axiom.

supporting_evidence.quote: 3. Check that no part of the proof relies on an unproven axiom introduced solely for convenience.

claim: New proof engineers reproduce a small archived proof under supervision during onboarding.

supporting_evidence.quote: 2. Reproduce a small, previously completed proof from the project's archive under supervision.

claim: Christodoulou's study of software verification through specification reduction supplies the layered-refinement terminology Vesper's documentation adopts.

supporting_evidence.quote: Christodoulou's study of επαλήθευση λογισμικού με αναγωγή προδιαγραφών (software verification through specification reduction) provides the layered-refinement terminology the project's documentation adopts.
